title: Year in Review
subtitle: Annual procurement summary — contracts, spending, and risk patterns
noData: No data available for this year

lede.pandemic: {{year}} was defined by the health emergency. With {{contracts}} contracts and {{directPct}}% awarded directly, emergency spending fundamentally reshaped public procurement patterns.
lede.transition: {{year}} was a year of presidential transition. {{contracts}} contracts totaling {{spending}} reflect the close of one administration and the start of another.
lede.highRisk: {{year}} recorded a high-risk rate of {{riskPct}}%, significantly above the OECD-recommended threshold. {{contracts}} contracts totaling {{spending}} warrant closer scrutiny.
lede.default: In {{year}}, the federal government processed {{contracts}} contracts totaling {{spending}}. The direct-award rate was {{directPct}}%.

topVendors.title: Top 10 Vendors by Value
topVendors.subtitle: Highest-value vendors in selected year
topVendors.limitedCoverage: Vendor ID coverage is limited for this period — only {{count}} providers identified.

topRiskVendors.subtitle: Vendors with highest average risk score that year (min 5 contracts)

sectorGrowth.subtitle: Year-over-year change in contract value by sector
sectorGrowth.noComparison: No prior-year data available for comparison

findings.headline: Statistical anomalies detected in {{year}}
findings.highRiskAboveOECD: High-risk rate of {{pct}}% — above the OECD 15% threshold
findings.highDirectAward: {{pct}}% of contracts awarded directly — concentration of discretionary power
findings.highSingleBid: {{pct}}% of competitive procedures received only a single bid
findings.spendingSpike: Spending increased {{pct}}% compared to the prior year
findings.spendingDrop: Spending dropped {{pct}}% compared to the prior year

riskBreakdown.subtitle: Distribution of contracts by risk level for selected year
riskBreakdown.noData: No risk data available for this year

yoy.label: YEAR OVER YEAR
yoy.subtitle: Change from prior year
yoy.firstYear: First year in dataset — no prior year comparison available

riskDist.label: RISK DISTRIBUTION
riskDist.yearEndNote: Historically, Mexican government procurement spikes in Q4 as agencies rush to exhaust annual budgets.

editorial.kicker: RUBLI · Annual Public Procurement Risk Report
editorial.body: Every year tells its own story of public spending. Here you will find the complete profile: who won, what it cost, which risk patterns dominated, and how it compares to the 2002-2025 historical average.

sectorAll.headline: All 12 sectors in {{year}} — complete spending distribution
sectorAll.subtitle: Total contracted value and contract count by sector. Click any bar to open the sector profile.
sectorAll.footerHint: Click any sector to open its detailed profile

sectorGrowthFull.headline: Which sectors grew and which contracted vs {{prior}}
sectorGrowthFull.subtitle: Year-over-year change in contracted value. Green = growth, red = contraction.
sectorGrowthFull.noComparison: No prior-year data ({{prior}}) available for comparison.

riskEvolution.headline: {{year}} risk profile vs historical average
riskEvolution.subtitle: Risk level distribution compared to the 2002-2025 average and the OECD threshold.
riskEvolution.verdictWorse: {{pct}} pp above historical average — a riskier year
riskEvolution.verdictBetter: {{pct}} pp below historical average — a cleaner year

procedureType.headline: Direct award vs competitive bidding
procedureType.subtitle: In {{year}}, {{directPct}}% of contracts were awarded directly, without formal competition.
procedureType.historicalContext: The 2002-2025 historical average is {{avg}}%. OECD recommends under 25%.

topVendorsFull.headline: The biggest vendors of {{year}}
topVendorsFull.subtitle: Ranked by total contracted value. RFC coverage is limited before 2018.

notableRisks.headline: The 10 highest-risk contracts in {{year}}
notableRisks.subtitle: Contracts with the highest risk scores under model v0.6.5. Not proof of wrongdoing — these are investigation priorities.
notableRisks.noRiskContracts: No high-risk contracts found for this year.

monthly.headline: When the money was spent in {{year}}
monthly.subtitle: Monthly distribution of contracted spending. December is colored amber — the year-end spike is a classic red flag.
monthly.decemberSpikeNote: {{pct}}% of annual spending concentrated in December — {{label}}
